draft.introduction: A processional hymn celebrating the King of glory entering through the gates. Likely connected to the liturgical procession of the Ark into the Temple.

translation.text: The earth is the Lord’s and the fullness thereof, the world and those who dwell therein. Who shall ascend the hill of the Lord? And who shall stand in his holy place? He who has clean hands and a pure heart.
translation.original_text: τοῦ κυρίου ἡ γῆ καὶ τὸ πλήρωμα αὐτῆς

translation.text: Lift up your heads, O gates! And be lifted up, O ancient doors, that the King of glory may come in. Who is this King of glory? The Lord, strong and mighty, the Lord, mighty in battle.

translator_notes.note: The patristic tradition read this psalm christologically: Christ ascends after the resurrection and the gates of heaven open to receive him. This reading appears in Justin Martyr and Irenaeus.
